tool_args.label: Metropolitan Transportation Authority
tool_args.raw_text: The Metropolitan Transportation Authority is North America's largest transportation network, serving a population of 15.3 million people across a 5,000-square-mile travel area.

payload.description: North America's largest transportation network, serving 15.3 million people across 5,000-square-mile travel area

tool_args.label: New York City Transit
tool_args.raw_text: New York City Transit and MTA Bus operate all subways and buses in New York City. Our 45,000 employees serve 4,500,000 passengers a day.

payload.description: Operates all subways and buses in New York City with MTA Bus. 45,000 employees serve 4,500,000 passengers a day. Nearly 6,700 subway cars, 5,800 buses, 472 subway stations, 640 miles of track, 27 bus depots, 70 shops and yards.

tool_args.label: Queens Bus Network Redesign
tool_args.raw_text: The Queens Bus Network Redesign team... delivered one of the largest and most complex bus network redesigns in North American history, implemented in two phases during the summer of 2025.

payload.description: One of the largest and most complex bus network redesigns in North American history, implemented in two phases during summer 2025. Introduced a modernized bus network for Queens. Six-year effort with three rounds of proposals, more than 18,000 comments, over 250 events.

tool_args.raw_text: Fresh Pond Depot achieving an increase of nearly 4,500 workdays

payload.description: Increase in workdays at Fresh Pond Depot attributed to Chaterpaul Singh's safety leadership

tool_args.label: Queens Bus Network Redesign Implementation
tool_args.raw_text: one of the largest and most complex bus network redesigns in North American history, implemented in two phases during the summer of 2025

payload.description: Queens Bus Network Redesign implemented in two phases during summer 2025, one of the largest bus network redesigns in North American history

tool_args.label: QBNR public engagement
tool_args.raw_text: The team engaged the Queens public through three rounds of proposals, collecting more than 18,000 comments and hosting or participating in over 250 events.

payload.claim_text: The Queens Bus Network Redesign team engaged the Queens public through three rounds of proposals, collecting more than 18,000 comments and hosting or participating in over 250 events.
payload.subject: Queens Bus Network Redesign

tool_args.label: QBNR Customer Ambassador Program
tool_args.raw_text: the Customer Ambassador program, which mobilized more than 750 employees across the MTA to staff over 3,000 shifts and distribute more than 600,000 brochures

payload.claim_text: The Customer Ambassador program mobilized more than 750 employees across the MTA to staff over 3,000 shifts and distribute more than 600,000 brochures.
payload.subject: Queens Bus Network Redesign

tool_args.label: QBNR brochures and inserts
tool_args.raw_text: Created more than 7,000 geographically tailored Guide-A-Ride inserts ... Eighteen neighborhood-specific brochures were developed in house in partnership with Marketing Production.

payload.claim_text: The Queens Bus Network Redesign team created more than 7,000 geographically tailored Guide-A-Ride inserts and 18 neighborhood-specific brochures developed in partnership with Marketing Production.
payload.subject: Queens Bus Network Redesign

tool_args.label: QBNR Final Plan book
tool_args.raw_text: coordinated the design and production of the 668-page Final Plan book, which was printed and distributed to Queens libraries, senior centers, and other key institutions

payload.claim_text: The Queens Bus Network Redesign team coordinated the design and production of the 668-page Final Plan book, printed and distributed to Queens libraries, senior centers, and other key institutions.
payload.subject: Queens Bus Network Redesign

tool_args.label: QBNR bus stop signage
tool_args.raw_text: The team also oversaw thousands of bus stop signage updates completed by the New York City Department of Transportation, along with additional street changes needed to support the redesigned network.

payload.claim_text: The team oversaw thousands of bus stop signage updates completed by the New York City Department of Transportation, along with additional street changes.
payload.subject: Queens Bus Network Redesign

tool_args.raw_text: Members of the New York City Transit Committee include: Haeda Mihaltses, Chair • Andrew Albert • Samuel Chu • Dan Garodnick • David Jones • Melva M. Miller • Meera Joshi • John Ross 'JR' Rizzo • John Samuelsen • Lisa Sorin • Midori Valdivia
